Sayed Ahmed is a Web Designer and Developer with contact information including email (sayed.ahmed@email.com), phone number (+20 101 234 5678), and links to his GitHub and LinkedIn profiles, as well as his personal website (sayedahmed.com).
Sayed Ahmed completed a Bachelor of Computer Application (B.C.A) from Pune University in 2017, preceded by his H.S.C. in 2015 and S.S.C. in 2013, both also from Pune University.

From August 2017 to the present, Sayed Ahmed has worked as a PHP Developer at Exposys Pvt. Ltd., where he developed and maintained web applications using PHP, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and MySQL, contributing to the full development lifecycle and collaborating with team members.
Sayed Ahmed developed the Pragat Bharat System, a data collection system for various sectors, using HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, PHP, JQuery, Ajax, and MySQL.
Sayed Ahmed contributed to the development of the Go Ayur System for Go Ayurveda Panchakarma center, utilizing HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, PHP, JQuery, Ajax, and MySQL.
Sayed Ahmed developed the Vitsanindia System, an online shopping application with a language change option, using HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, PHP, JQuery, Ajax, JavaScript, and MySQL.

Sayed Ahmed created the MahabaleshwarTours system, a tour management system with online registration, package information, ticket booking, and online payment facilities, utilizing HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, PHP, JQuery, Ajax, JavaScript, and MySQL.
Sayed Ahmed developed the Cityspaceindia website, a service provider website, using HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, PHP, JQuery, Ajax, JavaScript, and MySQL.

Sayed Ahmed designed and implemented the Fruitsbuddy system to manage fruit details, customers, orders, transactions, and payments, streamlining administrative tasks with HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, PHP, JQuery, Ajax, JavaScript, and MySQL.
Sayed Ahmed developed a real estate web application called Totalcitee.

A web-based user interface was developed for property sales, incorporating a search engine to efficiently display properties to visitors. The project utilized technologies including HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, PHP, JQuery, Ajax, JavaScript, and MySQL.

Golchha, a software developer working alone, created a website for a service provider, offering various service categories. The development utilized HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, PHP, JQuery, Ajax, JavaScript, and MySQL.

The developer possesses skills in web development using HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, PHP, Ajax, jQuery, JavaScript, and CodeIgniter, along with database expertise in MySQL. Development tools include Notepad++ and Sublime Text2, and the server environment consists of Apache Tomcat and Xampp Control Panel, running on Windows operating systems.

PHP is a versatile and widely-employed server-side scripting language, the backbone of a substantial portion of the web. It’s a pragmatic tool, favored by developers like Sayed Ahmed at Exposys Pvt. Ltd. for building and maintaining dynamic web applications – a craftsman consistently shaping online experiences since 2017. PHP isn’t a solitary entity; it thrives in collaboration. It’s the engine powering diverse systems born from Ahmed’s ingenuity: Pragat Bharat, a data collection hub; Go Ayur, facilitating Ayurvedic wellness; Vitsanindia, an adaptable online marketplace; MahabaleshwarTours, a comprehensive tour management platform; Cityspaceindia, connecting service providers; and Fruitsbuddy, a streamlined administrative tool for fruit vendors. 

Beyond Ahmed’s portfolio, PHP lends itself to projects both grand and individual, underpinning web-based property sales interfaces and smaller service provider websites alike. It’s a language comfortable with complexity, handling online payments, language switching, and intricate database interactions – often paired with MySQL for robust data storage. While capable of standing alone, PHP frequently works in concert with front-end technologies like HTML, CSS, JavaScript, jQuery, and Bootstrap, and frameworks like CodeIgniter, to deliver complete user experiences. Developers utilizing PHP often favor tools like Notepad++ or Sublime Text2 within a Xampp/Apache Tomcat environment on Windows, demonstrating its adaptability to common development workflows. PHP is, at its core, a facilitator – a powerful, flexible language enabling the creation of a vast and varied digital landscape.

JavaScript is a versatile and essential component of the modern web, a scripting language woven into the fabric of countless online experiences. It isn’t a solitary creator, but rather a collaborative partner, frequently found alongside PHP, HTML, CSS, and MySQL in the hands of developers like Sayed Ahmed at Exposys Pvt. Ltd. and the independent developer Golchha. 

It’s a builder of interactive storefronts – the Vitsanindia System’s language-switching capabilities and the Fruitsbuddy system’s streamlined management of fruit details, customers, and transactions all rely on its dynamic touch. It powers tour management, as seen in the MahabaleshwarTours system with its online registration and booking features, and breathes life into service provider websites like Cityspaceindia. 

JavaScript isn’t limited to consumer-facing applications; it also enhances administrative tools, such as the web-based user interface for property sales, providing efficient search functionality. Often working in tandem with libraries like JQuery and techniques like Ajax, it adds layers of responsiveness and user-friendliness. 

While comfortable within the Apache Tomcat and Xampp environments favored by many developers, and skillfully wielded with tools like Notepad++ and Sublime Text2, JavaScript’s true strength lies in its ubiquity – a silent, powerful force enabling the interactive web we know today. It’s a core skill for any web developer, a foundational element in bringing digital ideas to life.

AJAX is a quietly essential component in the modern web, a tireless worker behind the scenes enabling dynamic and responsive experiences. It isn’t a flashy front-end element, but rather the connective tissue that breathes life into websites, allowing them to update content without requiring a full page reload. Frequently found in the toolkit of developers like Sayed Ahmed and Golchha, AJAX consistently appears alongside technologies like HTML, CSS, PHP, JavaScript, and MySQL – a reliable partner in building everything from comprehensive data collection systems like Pragat Bharat, to online shopping platforms like Vitsanindia, and even tour management systems such as MahabaleshwarTours. 

It powers the subtle interactions users barely notice: the instant search results on a property sales website, the seamless updates within a fruit management system like Fruitsbuddy, and the dynamic content on service provider sites like Cityspaceindia and Go Ayur. AJAX isn’t a standalone creation, but a foundational element woven into the fabric of numerous web applications, consistently employed to enhance user experience and streamline functionality. It’s a core skill for web developers, facilitating a smoother, more interactive online world.

CSS is the silent architect of visual appeal, a foundational language consistently employed by Sayed Ahmed and Golchha in crafting the digital world. It’s rarely a standalone creation, but rather a crucial partner – the stylist to PHP’s structural engineer. From August 2017 onward, it has been a constant companion to Sayed Ahmed at Exposys Pvt. Ltd., shaping the user experience of countless web applications. 

It breathes life into systems like Pragat Bharat, Go Ayur, Vitsanindia, MahabaleshwarTours, Cityspaceindia, and Fruitsbuddy, dictating layout, color, and responsiveness. It’s the reason these platforms – a diverse collection ranging from data collection and Ayurvedic services to online shopping and tour management – *look* the way they do. 

CSS isn’t merely decorative; it’s integral to functionality. It’s the backbone of user interfaces, enabling efficient property searches and clear presentation of service categories. Often paired with Bootstrap for rapid development, it ensures a consistent and polished aesthetic across projects, making information accessible and engaging. While frequently overshadowed by the dynamic power of PHP, JavaScript, and databases like MySQL, CSS is the essential layer that transforms raw code into something visually coherent and user-friendly. It’s the quiet force behind a seamless digital experience.

MySQL is the quiet backbone of a bustling digital world, a relational database management system consistently called upon to structure and safeguard information. It isn’t a flashy front-end, but a dependable architect, providing the essential foundation for numerous web applications brought to life by developers like Sayed Ahmed. 

For over six years, Ahmed has consistently leveraged MySQL’s power at Exposys Pvt. Ltd., building and maintaining diverse web projects. It’s the silent partner in his creations – the Pragat Bharat System, a comprehensive data collector; the Go Ayur System for a Panchakarma center; the Vitsanindia online shopping platform with its multilingual capabilities; and the MahabaleshwarTours system, handling bookings and payments with ease. 

MySQL also underpins the Cityspaceindia service provider website, the streamlined Fruitsbuddy administrative system, and even a web-based property sales interface with its efficient search functionality. Even solo developers, like Golchha, rely on its stability for service provider websites. 

Essentially, MySQL is the trusted repository, diligently storing and organizing the data that fuels these applications – customer details, product catalogs, transaction histories, and everything in between. It’s a core skill for any PHP developer, working seamlessly alongside technologies like PHP, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and frameworks like CodeIgniter, often within environments like Xampp and Apache Tomcat. It doesn’t demand the spotlight, but without MySQL, these digital storefronts and systems simply wouldn’t function.

Bootstrap is a versatile and widely-adopted toolkit for crafting responsive, mobile-first web interfaces. It’s a foundational element favored by developers like Sayed Ahmed and Golchha, appearing as a core component in a diverse range of projects – from comprehensive systems like the Pragat Bharat data collection platform and the Go Ayur Ayurveda center’s management tool, to more focused applications such as Vitsanindia’s online shopping experience and MahabaleshwarTours’ tour booking service. 

It lends its structure to Cityspaceindia’s service provider website and the administrative backbone of Fruitsbuddy, streamlining the management of fruit details, customers, and transactions. Even a web-based property sales interface benefits from Bootstrap’s capabilities, ensuring a user-friendly search experience. 

Bootstrap isn’t merely a tool for large-scale projects; it empowers solo developers like Golchha to build professional-looking websites for service providers with relative ease. It consistently appears alongside HTML, CSS, PHP, JQuery, Ajax, JavaScript, and MySQL in these projects, suggesting a harmonious integration within a broader web development skillset. Essentially, Bootstrap is the reliable framework upon which many modern web experiences are built, providing a consistent and adaptable foundation for visual design and user interaction.

jQuery is a versatile and widely-adopted JavaScript library, a foundational tool in the hands of web developers like Sayed Ahmed and Golchha. It’s a nimble assistant, streamlining the complex tasks of manipulating web page content, handling events, performing animations, and interacting with Ajax – essentially, bringing dynamic life to static HTML. Not a standalone application itself, jQuery thrives as a component *within* larger systems. 

It’s a frequent collaborator in Ahmed’s projects: the data-driven Pragat Bharat System, the wellness-focused Go Ayur System, the e-commerce platform Vitsanindia, the tour management hub MahabaleshwarTours, the service directory Cityspaceindia, and the administrative tool Fruitsbuddy all benefit from its capabilities. Even a web-based property sales interface and a simple service provider website rely on jQuery to enhance user experience. 

Essentially, jQuery is the engine that powers interactivity. It’s often paired with HTML, CSS, PHP, Ajax, JavaScript, and MySQL, forming a robust toolkit for building modern web applications. While developers may also wield tools like CodeIgniter and environments like Apache Tomcat, jQuery remains a core skill, a reliable partner in crafting responsive and engaging online experiences. It doesn’t stand alone, but it’s rarely absent where dynamic web functionality is desired.

Sayed Ahmed is a resourceful and dedicated web designer and developer, steadily building a portfolio of practical applications since graduating with a B.C.A. from Pune University in 2017. He’s a hands-on professional currently employed as a PHP Developer at Exposys Pvt. Ltd., where he’s been a consistent contributor since August 2017, navigating the full development lifecycle and collaborating effectively with teams. 

He isn’t simply a coder, but a builder of solutions. Sayed has a knack for translating needs into functional web systems, demonstrated by his diverse projects. He architected the Pragat Bharat System for comprehensive data collection, and contributed to the Go Ayur System for Go Ayurveda Panchakarma center. He’s also brought online shopping to life with Vitsanindia, streamlined tour management with MahabaleshwarTours – complete with booking and payment features – and crafted a service provider platform in Cityspaceindia. 

Beyond these, Sayed designed Fruitsbuddy, a system for managing all aspects of a fruit business, and developed Totalcitee, a real estate web application. His toolkit is robust, encompassing PHP, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, MySQL, Bootstrap, JQuery, and Ajax, which he wields with proficiency to deliver polished, user-friendly experiences. 

Connect with Sayed Ahmed through his website (sayedahmed.com), or find him on LinkedIn and GitHub. He can also be reached via email at sayed.ahmed@email.com or by phone at +20 101 234 5678.

HTML is the foundational structure upon which much of Sayed Ahmed’s professional life is built. It’s the quiet architect behind a diverse portfolio of web applications and systems, consistently paired with CSS, Bootstrap, PHP, JavaScript, and MySQL to bring digital ideas to life. Not a flashy element itself, HTML provides the essential skeleton – the headings, paragraphs, forms, and links – for everything from the data-driven Pragat Bharat System, designed for broad sectoral data collection, to the specialized Go Ayur System for an Ayurveda center. 

It’s the common thread connecting Vitsanindia’s multilingual online shopping experience, MahabaleshwarTours’ comprehensive tour management platform, and the straightforward service presentation of Cityspaceindia. Even the more focused Fruitsbuddy system, meticulously tracking fruit details and transactions, and a web-based property sales interface rely on HTML’s core structure. 

Though often working within a team at Exposys Pvt. Ltd., and sometimes independently like Golchha, Sayed Ahmed consistently leverages HTML as the starting point, shaping user interfaces and enabling functionality across a spectrum of projects. It’s a reliable partner, always present, always forming the base layer of interaction for the digital world he creates. HTML isn’t the star, but without it, these systems simply wouldn’t exist.
